title: Introduction to Information Retrieval
text: Information retrieval is the activity of obtaining resources relevant to an information need.

title: BM25 Scoring
text: BM25 is a ranking function used by search engines to estimate the relevance of documents to a given search query.

title: Inverted Index
text: An inverted index is a database index storing a mapping from content to its locations in a table or document.

title: Term Frequency
text: Term frequency measures how frequently a term occurs in a document. The simplest choice is the raw count.

title: Document Frequency
text: Document frequency is the number of documents in the collection that contain a specific term.

title: TF-IDF Weighting
text: TF-IDF is a numerical statistic intended to reflect how important a word is to a document in a collection.

title: Precision and Recall
text: Precision is the fraction of retrieved documents that are relevant. Recall is the fraction of relevant documents that are retrieved.

title: NDCG Metric
text: Normalized discounted cumulative gain is a measure of ranking quality used in information retrieval.

title: BEIR Benchmark
text: BEIR is a heterogeneous benchmark containing diverse IR tasks for zero-shot evaluation of retrieval models.

title: Sparse Retrieval
text: Sparse retrieval methods like BM25 represent queries and documents as sparse vectors over the vocabulary.
